mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, equity discount rates decline and the present value of expected future cash flows rises, so the S&P 500 should generate positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns after downward FEDFUNDS trends are not positive more often than the unconditional base rate, or if the sign relation flips in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration combined with a high FEDFUNDS rate tightens real financial conditions and raises recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month median while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if returns are positive on average despite persistent inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and lower future sales, so when unemployment is trending up the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising UNRATE fail to produce negative average 60-day forward returns, or if the relationship is absent after excluding recession months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in PAYEMS indicates labor market deterioration and weaker consumer demand, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once payroll losses become visible.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200k over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll contraction episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears when revisions are ignored.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS reflects stronger nominal spending and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend RSAFS growth does not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is negative in a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while HOUST remains weak, the market is likely seeing supply-side industrial activity without broad housing-led demand support, a mix that should not sustain equity gains and should predict lower S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month average while HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up/HOUST-down regime is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals improving aggregate demand and corporate revenue prospects, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebound periods do not deliver positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is no stronger than during flat GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, falling RSAFS together with weak HOUST indicates real demand destruction and eroding pricing power, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation periods with falling RSAFS and weak HOUST do not underperform the unconditional 20-day return baseline, or if the sign becomes positive after controlling for CPI inflation.
